pi_items.source: Task input specification; 230V is standard grid voltage in many countries (Europe, India, Australia, etc.)
Task input specification; 14.5A is a realistic household/industrial load current
Task input specification; 0.82 is typical power factor for AC inductive loads (motors, etc.)
Independent calculation: P = V × I × PF = 230 × 14.5 × 0.82 = 2734.7 W ≈ 2.735 kW
Independent calculation: Q = √(S² - P²) = √(3335² - 2734.7²) = 1908.8 VAR ≈ 1.909 kVAR; confirmed via RapidTables and standard power factor calculation formulas
Independent calculation: S = V × I = 230 × 14.5 = 3335 VA = 3.335 kVA
Standard engineering tolerance; ±0.05 kW is reasonable for power calculation verification (±1.8% relative error)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic test data (hardcoded input values: 230V, 14.5A, 0.82 power factor) to test a single-phase AC power calculation. All ground truth values are mathematically correct: Active Power = 2.735 kW (V × I × PF), Apparent Power = 3.335 kVA (V × I), Reactive Power = 1.909 kVAR (√(S² - P²)). The tolerance of 0.05 kW is appropriate for power calculation verification. The app used (com.hsn.electricalcalculations) is a real, publicly available Android application on Google Play Store designed specifically for electrical engineering calculations, including single-phase and three-phase power calculations.
